vulnerability.title: Configure Logind to terminate idle sessions after certain time of inactivity
vulnerability.description: To configure logind service to terminate inactive user sessions after seconds, edit the file /etc/systemd/logind.conf. Ensure that there is a section [Login] which contains the configuration StopIdleSessionSec= .
vulnerability.recommendation: Remediation script: # Remediation is applicable only in certain platforms if rpm --quiet -q kernel-core && { ( grep -qP "^ID=[\"']?rhel[\"']?$" "/etc/os-release" && { real="$(grep -P "^VERSION_ID=[\"']?[\w.]+[\"']?$" /etc/os-release | sed "s/^VERSION_ID=[\"']\?\([^\"']\+\)[\"']\?$/\1/")"; expected="8.7"; printf "%s\n%s" "$expected" "$real" | sort -VC; } && grep -qP "^ID=[\"']?rhel[\"']?$" "/etc/os-release" && { real="$(grep -P "^VERSION_ID=[\"']?[\w.]+[\"']?$" /etc/os-release | sed "s/^VERSION_ID=[\"']\?\([^\"']\+\)[\"']\?$/\1/")"; expected="9.0"; [[ "$real" != "$expected" ]]; } ) || grep -qP "^ID=[\"']?ol[\"']?$" "/etc/os-release" && { real="$(grep -P "^VERSION_ID=[\"']?[\w.]+[\"']?$" /etc/os-release | sed "s/^VERSION_ID=[\"']\?\([^\"']\+\)[\"']\?$/\1/")"; expected="8.7"; printf "%s\n%s" "$expected" "$real"

triage_decision.reason: The remediation only edits /etc/systemd/logind.conf to set StopIdleSessionSec=, which is a standard, low‑impact configuration change that does not affect authentication, networking, or critical boot components. It does not risk locking out users or breaking services.
triage_decision.estimated_impact: minor configuration change; requires service restart

#IdleAction=ignore
#RuntimeDirectorySize=10%
#RuntimeDirectoryInodesMax=
#RemoveIPC=no
#InhibitorsMax=8192
#SessionsMax=8192

# the logind.conf.d/ subdirectory. The latter is generally recommended.
# Defaults can be restored by simply deleting this file and all drop-ins.
#
# Use 'systemd-analyze cat-config systemd/logind.conf' to display the full config.
#
# See logind.conf(5) for details.

[Login]
StopIdleSessionSec=10min
#NAutoVTs=6
#ReserveVT=6
#KillUserProcesses=no
#KillOnlyUsers=
#KillExcludeUsers=root
#InhibitDelayMaxSec=5
#UserStopDelaySec=10

#SuspendKeyIgnoreInhibited=no
#HibernateKeyIgnoreInhibited=no
#LidSwitchIgnoreInhibited=yes
#RebootKeyIgnoreInhibited=no
#HoldoffTimeoutSec=30s
#IdleAction=ignore

#RuntimeDirectoryInodesMax=

llm_verdict.message:
The remediation has been applied:

1. **Backup** – Created `/etc/systemd/logind.conf.bak` to preserve the original file.
2. **Configuration** – The file already contains the correct setting:
   ```
   [Login]
   StopIdleSessionSec=10min
   ```
   No further modifications were needed.
3. **Service Update** – Reloaded/restarted `systemd-logind` with:
   ```
   systemctl try-reload-or-restart systemd-logind
   ```

The system now correctly terminates idle sessions after 10 minutes, satisfying the **logind_session_timeout** rule.

previous_review_verdicts.feedback: The plan correctly modifies logind.conf to set StopIdleSessionSec and reloads the daemon, which will remediate the issue. Minor improvements around idempotency and exact value syntax are recommended.
concerns: The plan does not verify whether the [Login] section already exists before blindly adding another one.
It uses 'systemctl reload systemd‑logged?; this may not fully apply the change on all systems; a restart might be required.
suggested_improvements: Check pre‑existing [Login] section and add the line only if it not already present.
Use systemctl restart systemd‑logind instead of reload to guarantee the new setting that …

previous_review_verdicts.feedback: The plan correctly ensures the StopIdleSessionSec setting is present under the [Login] section and applies the change using try-reload-or-restart, which will activate the configuration. It addresses the concerns raised in the prior review and will remediate the vulnerability.
concerns: Potential for multiple [Login] sections if the file already contains more than one, leading to duplicate headers.
No backup of the original logind.conf before modification.
The plan does not explicitly check whether the current value already matches the desired one, which could cause unnecessary writes.
suggested_improvements: Create a backup of /etc/systemd/logind.conf before editing (e.g., cp /etc/systemd/logind.conf /etc/systemd/logind.conf.bak).
If multiple [Login] sections exist, consolidate to a single section or modify the first occurrence to avoid duplication.
Add a check to skip rewriting the file when StopIdleSessionSec is already set to the intended value.
Consider using `systemctl restart systemd-logind` explicitly for systems where try-reload-or-restart might not reload the configuration.
